description: Calendar Sync for Burn Buddies & Squads — Add to Calendar button that downloads .ics files with recurring workout schedules

userStories.title: Create ICS calendar generation utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a utility function that generates valid RFC 5545 .ics content from a workout schedule so both buddy and squad calendar endpoints can reuse it.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a utility module (e.g., services/api/src/lib/ics-generator.ts) that exports a function to generate .ics content
Function accepts: schedule days array, optional time (HH:MM), event title string, and returns a valid .ics string
Maps WorkoutSchedule.days (Mon, Tue, etc.) to iCalendar BYDAY values (MO, TU, WE, TH, FR, SA, SU)
When time is provided, creates timed VEVENT entries with DTSTART at that time (UTC) and 1-hour DTEND
When no time is provided, creates all-day VEVENT entries using VALUE=DATE
Each VEVENT includes RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=<mapped-days> for weekly recurrence
Each VEVENT includes a VALARM with TRIGGER:-PT30M (30-minute reminder before event)
Generated .ics output starts with BEGIN:VCALENDAR and ends with END:VCALENDAR
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Consider using the ical-generator npm package or hand-rolling the .ics string — the format is simple enough. WorkoutSchedule type is in @burnbuddy/shared.

userStories.title: Add burn buddy calendar endpoint with tests
userStories.description: As a user, I want to download an .ics file for my burn buddy's workout schedule so I can add it to my calendar app.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /burn-buddies/:id/calendar endpoint exists on the burn-buddies router
Endpoint uses requireAuth middleware
Returns Content-Type: text/calendar and Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="burnbuddy-workout.ics"
Fetches the BurnBuddy document and partner's UserProfile.displayName from Firestore via getDb()
Event title format: '🔥 Workout with [partner displayName]'
Uses the ICS generation utility from US-001 to build the .ics content
Returns 404 if buddy not found or requesting user is not a member
Returns 400 if no workout schedule is configured (empty or missing days)
Unit tests cover: valid schedule with time, valid schedule without time, no schedule configured, unauthorized access (user not a member)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Follow existing API test pattern: vi.hoisted + vi.mock for firebase/firestore, buildApp(), supertest. The partner displayName requires a Firestore lookup on the users collection.

userStories.title: Add burn squad calendar endpoint with tests
userStories.description: As a squad member, I want to download an .ics file for my squad's workout schedule so I can add it to my calendar app.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /burn-squads/:id/calendar endpoint exists on the burn-squads router
Endpoint uses requireAuth middleware
Returns Content-Type: text/calendar and Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="burnbuddy-workout.ics"
Fetches the BurnSquad document from Firestore via getDb()
Event title format: '🔥 [squad name] Workout'
Uses the ICS generation utility from US-001 to build the .ics content
Returns 404 if squad not found or requesting user is not a member
Returns 400 if no workout schedule configured in squad settings
Unit tests cover: valid schedule with time, valid schedule without time, no schedule configured, unauthorized access (user not a member)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Follow existing API test pattern. Squad schedule lives in BurnSquad.settings.workoutSchedule. Squad name comes from BurnSquad.name directly.

userStories.title: Add 'Add to Calendar' button on buddy detail page
userStories.description: As a user viewing my burn buddy's detail page, I want an 'Add to Calendar' button so I can download our workout schedule as a calendar file.
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Add to Calendar' button with a calendar icon (📅 or SVG) appears in the schedule section of the buddy detail page
Button is only visible when a workout schedule is configured (non-empty days array)
Clicking the button calls GET /burn-buddies/:id/calendar with the auth Bearer header via fetch()
Download is triggered via blob + temporary object URL pattern (not a direct <a> link, since auth headers are required)
While downloading, the button shows a loading/spinner state
If the download fails, a toast or inline error message is shown to the user
Button uses the same styling as other action buttons on the page (consistent Tailwind classes)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use the existing api.ts helper (getIdToken + Bearer header) for the fetch call. Create a blob from the response and use URL.createObjectURL + temporary anchor click to trigger download.

userStories.title: Add 'Add to Calendar' button on squad detail page
userStories.description: As a squad member viewing the squad detail page, I want an 'Add to Calendar' button so I can add our squad workout schedule to my calendar.
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Add to Calendar' button with a calendar icon (📅 or SVG) appears in the schedule/settings section of the squad detail page
Button is only visible when a workout schedule is configured in squad settings (non-empty days array)
Clicking the button calls GET /burn-squads/:id/calendar with the auth Bearer header via fetch()
Download is triggered via blob + temporary object URL pattern
While downloading, the button shows a loading/spinner state
If the download fails, a toast or inline error message is shown to the user
Button uses the same styling as other action buttons on the page (consistent Tailwind classes)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Same download pattern as US-004 but targeting the squad calendar endpoint. Consider extracting a shared AddToCalendarButton component if the buddy and squad buttons are identical.
